theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Consumer Staples (+2.27%) and Health Care (+2.02%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.30%) and Industrials (-0.48%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.43% versus +1.32% for defensives and +0.41% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.32%, Dow -0.03%, Nasdaq -0.95% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up +0.10%, outperforming large caps. The VIX rose 4.28% to 16.34, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.14% to $3,994.60, crude oil was little changed at $78.85, and bitcoin slipped 0.44% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
